title: Laptop and Mobile Components, Replacement, and Physical Service · Changed-Device Lab
summary: A different user, device, environment, symptom, constraint, and changed condition requires an independent transfer of laptop and mobile components, replacement, and physical service into a new mobile hardware serviceability and repair decision. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of mobile hardware serviceability and repair decision with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, a…
objectives: Map batteries, displays, digitizers, cameras, microphones, speakers, keyboards, touchpads, antennas, storage, memory, wireless cards, ports, and replaceable units.
Use exact model identity, service documentation, fastener and cable maps, tool requirements, warranty, parts, and reassembly checkpoints.
Trace adapter, cable, negotiated power, port, charge controller, battery state, firmware, thermal conditions, and operating-system reporting.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# comptia-a-plus cumulative fictional support evidence portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic text-contract review only. They never execute learner
# commands, inspect the host, touch devices or accounts, connect to networks, open equipment,
# handle malware, erase media, contact people, or claim certification.
workspace: support
ticket-id: fictional-ticket
user-impact: [replace with user outcome, scope, severity, and urgency]
environment: [replace with asset, version, configuration, owner, dependency, and evidence time]
safety-authority: [replace with consent, hazards, data custody, privacy, access, and stop rules]
baseline: [replace with expected behavior, known-good state, conditions, and measurement]
symptom: [replace with observed behavior, reproduction, frequency, timing, and evidence]
recent-change: [replace with timeline, relevant changes, counterevidence, and unknowns]
hypothesis: [replace with falsifiable cause, competing explanation, prediction, and confidence]
test: [replace with one safe reversible discriminating test, changed case, and stop rule]
observation: [replace with actual result, provenance, rejected inference, and next decision]
repair-rollback: [replace with least-change repair, authority, backup, trigger, and restored state]
verify: [replace with user outcome, original, changed, side-effect, restart, and recurrence checks]
prevention: [replace with prevention, monitoring, owner, review date, and residual risk]
escalation: [replace with stop threshold, destination, evidence packet, and requested decision]
documentation: [replace with plain structured status, redaction, access, handoff, and uncertainty]
transfer-boundary: authorized disposable equipment or simulator only
review:

steps.title: CompTIA A+ support Commit to the mission model · Lab Mobile Component Map
steps.instruction:
lab case — A thin laptop has cracked glass, unreliable charging, a damaged hinge, possible battery swelling, urgent data needs, and uncertain service documentation. The lab variation 3 changes the user outcome, device, version, hazard, recent change, symptom, access need, competing cause, and transfer limit. Competency probe: defend one safe causal support decision

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: CompTIA A+ support Commit to the mission model · Lab Mobile Component Map.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for map batteries, displays, digitizers, cameras, microphones, speakers, keyboards, touchpads, antennas, storage, memory, wireless cards, ports, and replaceable units. For CompTIA A+ support Commit to the mission model · Lab Mobile Component Map, keep Mobile Component Map connected to mechanically reviewable user-impact, asset, safety, baseline, symptom, hypothesis, test, observation, repair, rollback, verification, escalation, documentation, and transfer decision evidence.

content.text: A thin laptop has cracked glass, unreliable charging, a damaged hinge, possible battery swelling, urgent data needs, and uncertain service documentation. The lab variation 3 changes the user outcome, device, version, hazard, recent change, symptom, access need, competing cause, and transfer limit. Competency probe: defend one safe causal support decision

content.title: Commit before feedback
content.text: Choose from the stated data before opening the worked model. Record which Mobile Component Map condition decided your prediction.

content.label: Authority to consult after committing
content.value: CompTIA A+ Core 1 220-1201 Exam Objectives — Controls every current Core 1 mobile, networking, hardware, virtualization, cloud, and troubleshooting objective and weighting.

hints: For CompTIA A+ support Commit to the mission model · Lab Mobile Component Map, name the user impact, fictional ticket, asset, environment, authorization, hazard, baseline, symptom, timeline, hypothesis, test, observation, repair, rollback, verification, prevention, escalation, accessible documentation, and transfer boundary, Mobile Component Map precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At CompTIA A+ support Commit to the mission model · Lab Mobile Component Map, trace this concrete input and predict user impact, safety and authority, baseline, reproduction, timeline, hypothesis, bounded test, observation, rejection, repair, rollback, original and changed-case verification, prevention, escalation, and controlled transfer evidence before reviewing it: A thin laptop has cracked glass, unreliable charging, a damaged hinge, possible battery swelling, urgent data needs, and uncertain service documentation. The lab variation 3 changes the user outcome, device, version, hazard, recent change, symptom, access need, competing cause, and transfer limit. Competency probe: defend one safe causal support decision

For CompTIA A+ support Commit to the mission model · Lab Mobile Component Map, challenge the documented misconception with changed-user, device, version, hazard, baseline, symptom, timeline, access need, hypothesis, test result, repair, restart, recurrence, escalation, and controlled equipment-transfer evidence: Every laptop component is a smaller desktop component with the same replacement path.

options.text: State the Mobile Component Map preconditions, preserve earlier contracts, and name the trace or changed-input result that will decide success.
Every laptop component is a smaller desktop component with the same replacement path. That claim fails the commit to the mission model evidence boundary for Mobile Component Map.

For commit to the mission model, apply one familiar repair, omit user impact, authorization, safety, asset and version identity, baseline, recent change, competing cause, prediction, observation, rollback, changed-case verification, accessibility, privacy, escalation, and controlled transfer, then accept a missing symptom as support proof. This does not satisfy Mobile Component Map for mobile hardware serviceability and repair decision.

lines.label: assumption
